book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XXV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: One who had betrothed a maiden, corrupted her sister, so that she conceived.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: After that he married his betrothed, but she who had been corrupted hanged herself.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The parties to this affair were ordered to be received among the co-standers after ten years [of penance] according to the prescribed degrees.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: A certain body after being engaged to marry a young girl, violates her sister and then takes her to wife.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: All who were cognizant of the affair are to be subject to penance for ten years.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: I have followed the usual translation “hanged herself,” which is the ordinary dictionary-meaning of ἀπάγχω, but Hefele says that it signifies any and every variety of suicides.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: In this case we have many nefarious crimes committed, fornication, unlawful marriage [i.e. with the sister of one’s mistress] and murder.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: In that case [mentioned by St. Basil in Canon lxxviij. where only seven years penance is enjoined] there is only a nefarious marriage [i.e. with a wife’s sister].
